creator: vsjcfm@gmail.com
text:
> please try again with the command

> nmap --unprivileged $target

root@jason:~# nmap --unprivileged -v scanme.nmap.org

Starting Nmap 5.51 ( http://nmap.org ) at 2011-02-22 12:01 EET
Initiating Ping Scan at 12:01
Scanning scanme.nmap.org (64.13.134.52) [2 ports]
Completed Ping Scan at 12:01, 3.04s elapsed (1 total hosts)
Initiating Parallel DNS resolution of 1 host. at 12:01
Completed Parallel DNS resolution of 1 host. at 12:01, 0.00s elapsed
Initiating Connect Scan at 12:01
Scanning scanme.nmap.org (64.13.134.52) [1000 ports]
Discovered open port 22/tcp on 64.13.134.52
Discovered open port 80/tcp on 64.13.134.52
Discovered open port 53/tcp on 64.13.134.52
Connect Scan Timing: About 28.30% done; ETC: 12:03 (0:01:19 remaining)
Connect Scan Timing: About 63.45% done; ETC: 12:03 (0:00:35 remaining)
Completed Connect Scan at 12:03, 91.72s elapsed (1000 total ports)
Nmap scan report for scanme.nmap.org (64.13.134.52)
Host is up (0.20s latency).
Not shown: 993 filtered ports
PORT      STATE  SERVICE
22/tcp    open   ssh
25/tcp    closed smtp
53/tcp    open   domain
70/tcp    closed gopher
80/tcp    open   http
113/tcp   closed auth
31337/tcp closed Elite

Read data files from: /usr/local/share/nmap
Nmap done: 1 IP address (1 host up) scanned in 94.82 seconds


root@jason:~# nmap --unprivileged -v -sS scanme.nmap.org
You requested a scan type which requires root privileges.
QUITTING!


root@jason:~# nmap -v --send-eth scanme.nmap.org

Starting Nmap 5.51 ( http://nmap.org ) at 2011-02-22 12:06 EET
nexthost: failed to determine route to scanme.nmap.org (64.13.134.52)
QUITTING!


root@jason:~# nmap -v --send-ip scanme.nmap.org

Starting Nmap 5.51 ( http://nmap.org ) at 2011-02-22 12:07 EET
nexthost: failed to determine route to scanme.nmap.org (64.13.134.52)
QUITTING!


root@jason:~# nmap -v --send-ip -e ng0 scanme.nmap.org

Starting Nmap 5.51 ( http://nmap.org ) at 2011-02-22 12:07 EET
nexthost: failed to determine route to scanme.nmap.org (64.13.134.52)


Yes, nmap can scan over ng interface. But in unprivileged mode only
TCP Connect Scan available, I cannot use SYN Stealth or any other
types.
